title: Quarterly Internal Scan Remediation Aging Buckets

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 6.3.1 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 6.3.1 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.11.79: Quarterly Internal Scan Remediation Aging Buckets.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.79' running on index=vm qualys:host with FIRST_FOUND_DATETIME, STATUS, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

spl:
index=vm sourcetype="qualys:host" earliest=-120d
| where match(TAGS,"(?i)PCI|CDE)")
| where match(STATUS,"(?i)active|new|reopened)")
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(FIRST_FOUND_DATETIME,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))/86400,1)
| eval bucket=case(age_days<=30,"0-30d", age_days<=60,"31-60d", age_days<=90,"61-90d",1=1,"90+d")
| stats count by bucket, SEVERITY_LEVEL
| sort bucket
description: Buckets open internal scan findings by age so quarterly rescan and fix cycles for high-risk issues remain on schedule and visible to management dashboards.
value: Buckets open internal scan findings by age so quarterly rescan and fix cycles for high-risk issues remain on schedule and visible to management dashboards.
implementation: (1) Confirm Qualys timestamp format for `strptime`. (2) Map `SEVERITY_LEVEL` to PCI risk. (3) Executive stack chart weekly. (4) Align `90+d` alerts with remediation charter.
visualization: Stacked column (bucket x severity), pie (90+d share), table (raw host list drilldown).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: Buckets open internal scan findings by age so quarterly rescan and fix cycles for high-risk issues remain on schedule and visible to management dashboards ‚Äî so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=vm` `qualys:host` with `FIRST_FOUND_DATETIME`, `STATUS`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Confirm Qualys timestamp format for `strptime`. (2) Map `SEVERITY_LEVEL` to PCI risk. (3) Executive stack chart weekly. (4) Align `90+d` alerts with remediation charter.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vm sourcetype="qualys:host" earliest=-120d
| where match(TAGS,"(?i)PCI|CDE)")
| where match(STATUS,"(?i)active|new|reopened)")
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(FIRST_FOUND_DATETIME,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))/86400,1)
| eval bucket=case(age_days<=30,"0-30d", age_days<=60,"31-60d", age_days<=90,"61-90d",1=1,"90+d")
| stats count by bucket, SEVERITY_LEVEL
| sort bucket
```

Understanding this SPL

**Quarterly Internal Scan Remediation Aging Buckets (PCI DSS Req 11.3.1.1, 6.3.1)** ‚Äî Buckets open internal scan findings by age so quarterly rescan and fix cycles for high-risk issues remain on schedule and visible to management dashboards.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vm` `qualys:host` with `FIRST_FOUND_DATETIME`, `STATUS`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vm; **sourcetype**: qualys:host. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=vm, sourcetype="qualys:host", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(TAGS,"(?i)PCI|CDE)")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(STATUS,"(?i)active|new|reopened)")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **bucket** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by bucket, SEVERITY_LEVEL** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Quarterly Internal Scan Remediation Aging Buckets (PCI DSS Req 11.3.1.1, 6.3.1)** ‚Äî Buckets open internal scan findings by age so quarterly rescan and fix cycles for high-risk issues remain on schedule and visible to management dashboards.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vm` `qualys:host` with `FIRST_FOUND_DATETIME`, `STATUS`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked column (bucket x severity), pie (90+d share), table (raw host list drilldown).
